name: ComponentRules
description: Component creation and modification rules for Harmony Design System

atomicDesign.description: Component complexity hierarchy following Atomic Design principles
atomicDesign.note: Harmony uses functional categories (form, display, navigation) rather than atomic terminology in naming, but understanding atomic levels helps guide composition and complexity decisions

rules.rule: Components should align with appropriate complexity level
rules.rationale: Helps determine scope, dependencies, and reusability of components

atoms.guidance: Atoms should have no dependencies on other Harmony components

molecules.guidance: Molecules should depend only on atoms, not other molecules or organisms

organisms.description: Complex components composed of molecules and/or atoms
organisms.guidance: Organisms can depend on molecules and atoms. Avoid organism depending on organism.

templates.guidance: Templates orchestrate organisms, molecules, and atoms into complete layouts

rules.rule: Respect component dependency hierarchy
rules.rationale: Prevents circular dependencies and maintains clear architecture

examples.correct: Input (molecule) depends on Label (atom) and Icon (atom)

rules.rule: Higher complexity = lower reusability is expected
rules.rationale: Atoms are used everywhere, organisms are more specific

guidance.atoms: Should be usable in 80%+ of contexts
guidance.molecules: Should be usable in 50%+ of contexts
guidance.organisms: May be specific to 10-20% of contexts

rules.rule: Maximum 6-9 variants per component
rules.rationale: Button has 6 variants (primary, secondary, tertiary, outline, ghost, destructive), Badge has 9. This is the acceptable range to prevent variant explosion.

examples.correct: Button variants: primary, secondary, tertiary, outline, ghost, destructive
examples.incorrect: Creating variants like 'lightBlue', 'mediumBlue', 'darkBlue' - use themes instead

rules.rule: Variants must have semantic meaning
rules.rationale: Variants should describe purpose or state, not visual attributes

examples.correct: primary, secondary, success, error, warning, info

rules.rule: Use consistent variant names across components
rules.rationale: Maintains predictable API across the design system

rules.rule: Always use Icon component, never inline SVG
rules.rationale: Icon component provides consistent sizing, theming, and accessibility

rules.rule: Import Icon component when using icons
rules.rationale: 34 out of 49 components use Icon - it's the standard pattern

rules.rule: Support iconPosition prop for buttons/links
rules.rationale: Allows flexible icon placement (left or right)

rules.rule: Provide sensible default icons for semantic variants
rules.rationale: Alert component maps variant to default icon (info→information-circle, success→check-circle)

examples.correct: const defaultIcons = { info: 'information-circle', success: 'check-circle', warning: 'exclamation-triangle', error: 'exclamation-circle' };
examples.when: Component has semantic variants (success, error, warning, info)

rules.rule: Use standard size scale: xs, sm, md, lg
rules.rationale: Consistent sizing across all components

sizes.xs: Extra small (e.g., Button xs, Badge xs)

rules.rule: Default size should be 'md'
rules.rationale: Provides consistent middle-ground sizing

rules.rule: Map component size to icon size appropriately
rules.rationale: Icons should scale proportionally with component

examples.correct: const iconSizeMap = { xs: 'xs', sm: 'sm', md: 'sm', lg: 'md' }

rules.rule: All components must accept class prop
rules.rationale: Allows custom styling and composition

rules.rule: Interactive components must support disabled prop
rules.rationale: Standard pattern for form controls and buttons

rules.rule: Form inputs should support error and errorMessage props
rules.rationale: Consistent error handling across form components

rules.rule: Form inputs should support required prop
rules.rationale: Standard HTML form validation

rules.rule: Use rest spread for additional HTML attributes
rules.rationale: Allows passing arbitrary HTML attributes
rules.pattern:
[key: string]: any; // in Props interface
const { ...rest } = Astro.props;

composition.description: Rules for component composition and nesting

rules.rule: Use named slots for complex layouts
rules.rationale: Card uses header, header-actions, footer slots for flexible composition

examples.when: Component has distinct sections (header, body, footer, actions)

rules.rule: Choose slots for content, props for configuration
rules.rationale: Slots are flexible for content, props are type-safe for configuration

useSlots: Content that varies significantly between uses
Need to pass complex JSX/HTML structure
Content includes multiple elements or components
User needs full control over markup

examples.slots: Card header slot accepts any content (headings, icons, buttons)
examples.props: Button variant prop accepts specific enum values ('primary' | 'secondary')
examples.hybrid: Input accepts label prop (string) OR uses Label component (composition)

rules.rule: Use consistent, descriptive slot names
rules.rationale: Predictable API across components
rules.note: Default slot should be for primary content

rules.rule: Use compound component pattern for tightly coupled components
rules.rationale: Provides flexible composition while maintaining relationship
rules.when: Components that must be used together but need flexibility in ordering/content

examples.pattern: Accordion + AccordionItem, Stepper + Step, TabStrip + Tab
examples.benefit: Parent manages state, children provide content

rules.implementation: Parent component provides context, child components consume context

rules.rule: Import dependent components at the top
rules.rationale: Clear dependencies, following Alert pattern (imports Icon, Button, ProgressBar)

rules.rule: Avoid components that require deep nesting
rules.rationale: Keep composition simple and maintainable
rules.guidance: If composition requires >3 levels, consider flattening or creating intermediate components

rules.rule: Prefer composition over complex configuration
rules.rationale: Allow users to compose with slots rather than passing complex config objects
rules.when: Configuration object has >3 properties or nested objects

rules.rule: Create wrapper components for common compositions
rules.rationale: Reduces repetition, encapsulates patterns

examples.pattern: If Input + Label used together frequently, create FormField wrapper
examples.benefit: FormField handles Label + Input + error display consistently

rules.note: Harmony's Input component already includes Label - this is the wrapper pattern

rules.rule: Interactive components should support loading state
rules.rationale: Button has loading prop + loadingText for async actions

rules.rule: Use boolean props for state modifiers
rules.rationale: Card uses elevated, interactive, primary as boolean flags
rules.when: State is on/off rather than multiple options

rules.rule: Add ARIA attributes for state changes
rules.rationale: Button uses aria-busy for loading state, Dialog uses aria-modal

rules.rule: Use semantic HTML elements
rules.rationale: Better accessibility and SEO

rules.rule: Provide aria-label for icon-only buttons
rules.rationale: Screen readers need text alternative

examples.reference: Dialog close button, Alert dismiss button

rules.rule: All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible
rules.rationale: Tab navigation and Enter/Space activation required

examples.dialog: Escape key closes, focus trap within dialog

rules.rule: Provide visible focus indicators
rules.rationale: Keyboard users need visual feedback

rules.rule: Use BEM-style class naming
rules.rationale: Consistent, predictable class structure

rules.rule: Compose classes using array.filter(Boolean).join(' ')
rules.rationale: Clean way to conditionally add classes

rules.rule: Avoid inline styles, use CSS classes and tokens
rules.rationale: Maintains theme consistency and separation of concerns
exceptions: Dynamic values that cannot be predetermined

rules.rule: Use CSS custom properties for theming
rules.rationale: Enables theme switching and dark mode

antiPatterns.why: Breaks consistency, makes icons hard to update
antiPatterns.instead: Use Icon component with name prop

antiPatterns.pattern: Creating custom size names (tiny, huge, massive)
antiPatterns.why: Breaks size consistency across design system

antiPatterns.pattern: Creating 10+ variants for a single component
antiPatterns.why: Makes API complex, hard to maintain

antiPatterns.pattern: Variant names describing visuals (blue, rounded, shadowed)
antiPatterns.why: Not semantic, breaks when themes change

antiPatterns.pattern: Passing deeply nested config objects as props
antiPatterns.why: Hard to type, document, and use

bestPractices.practice: Add JSDoc comment at top of component file
bestPractices.example:
/**
 * Button Component
 *
 * Interactive button with multiple variants, sizes, and states.
 */
bestPractices.rationale: Improves developer experience and IDE support
Type safety and autocomplete in consuming code

bestPractices.practice: Provide sensible defaults for all optional props
bestPractices.example: const { variant = 'primary', size = 'md', disabled = false } = Astro.props;

bestPractices.practice: Start with base functionality, add enhancements progressively
bestPractices.example: Alert has 'default' and 'enhanced' styles - default works everywhere
